title: grok-workflow.yaml
description: Official JSON Schema for grok-workflow.yaml — defines multi-step automated workflows with conditional branching, error handling, and composable actions. Compatible with grok-install.yaml@1.0+ and grok@2026.4+.

version.description: Semantic version of this workflow configuration file.

author.description: X handle of the workflow config owner, prefixed with '@'.

compatibility.description: Platform specs this file is tested and compatible with.

workflows.description: Named workflow definitions. Each key is the workflow identifier used in '@grok run workflow:<Name>' triggers.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this workflow accomplishes end-to-end.

trigger.description: Canonical @grok trigger string used to invoke this workflow from any X context.

timeout_minutes.description: Maximum wall-clock minutes the entire workflow may run before being force-stopped.

on_failure.description: Global failure strategy applied when any step fails and the step has no local on_error override.

notify_on_complete.description: Post an X notification when the workflow finishes (success or failure).

orchestration.description: Optional multi-agent orchestration metadata. When present, the workflow coordinates one or more sub-agents under a declared execution mode.

mode.description: Execution topology for the orchestrated agents.

agents.description: Sub-agents participating in this orchestration. Each entry describes a named role and the tools it may invoke.

name.description: Identifier used to reference this agent in graph edges and step outputs.

role.description: Human-readable description of the agent's responsibility in this orchestration.

tools.description: Tool identifiers this agent is permitted to invoke. Must match entries in grok-tools.yaml.

count.description: Number of parallel instances of this agent to spawn. Useful for crew and debate_swarm modes.

graph.description: Directed execution graph. Used when mode is 'graph' or 'hybrid'.

edges.description: Directed edges between agents, optionally guarded by a condition expression.

from.description: Source agent name — must match an agent 'name' defined in this orchestration.

to.description: Target agent name — must match an agent 'name' defined in this orchestration.

condition.description: JavaScript-style boolean expression gating this edge. Edge is traversed only when the expression evaluates to true.

debate_swarm.description: Debate-swarm settings. Used when mode is 'debate_swarm'.

rounds.description: Number of debate rounds before a final vote is tallied.

voter_model.description: Model used to adjudicate the final vote across debate participants.

quorum.description: Fraction of voters that must agree for a motion to pass.

memory.description: Shared memory configuration for the orchestrated agents.

type.description: Memory backend class used across the orchestration.

persist.description: Persist memory contents across workflow runs. Only meaningful when type is 'long_term' or 'vector'.

vector_store.description: Vector database provider. Required when type is 'vector'.

steps.description: Ordered sequence of actions executed top-to-bottom. Steps share a runtime context and can reference previous step outputs.

name.description: Short display name for this step shown in workflow progress output.

action.description: Action to execute. Can be a grok spec name (e.g. 'grok-test'), a tool identifier, or a prompt library key.

input.description: Static input string passed to the action (e.g. 'all' for grok-test).

template.description: Prompt library key to use when the action is 'grok-prompts' or 'publish_thread'.

prompt.description: Prompt library key to use when action is 'grok-prompts'.

condition.description: JavaScript-style boolean expression evaluated against previous step outputs. Step is skipped when false.

on_error.description: Error handling strategy for this specific step. Overrides the workflow-level on_failure setting.

retry_count.description: Number of automatic retries on transient failure before triggering on_error. Only used when on_error is 'retry'.

timeout_minutes.description: Per-step timeout in minutes. Overrides the workflow-level timeout for this step.

env.description: Environment variables made available to this step's action.
